questionHtml: Determine the mass, in g, of CaCO3 (s) produced by reacting 2.41 dm3 of 2.33 × 10−2 mol dm−3 of Ca(OH)2 (aq) with 0.750 dm3 of CO2 (g) at STP.
markschemeHtml:
«nCa(OH)2 = 2.41 dm3 × 2.33 × 10−2 mol dm−3 =» 0.0562 «mol» AND

«nCO2 =0.750 dm322.7 mol dm-3=» 0.0330 «mol» ✓

«CO2 is the limiting reactant»

«mCaCO3 = 0.0330 mol × 100.09 g mol−1 =» 3.30 «g» ✓

 

Only award ECF for M2 if limiting reagent is used.

Accept answers in the range 3.30 - 3.35 «g».

meta.title: Determine the mass, in g, of CaCO3 (s) produced by reacting 2.41 dm3 of 2.33 × 10−2 mol dm−3 of Ca(OH)2 (aq) with 0.750 dm3 of CO2 (g) at STP.
breadcrumbLabels: Structure 1. Models of the particulate nature of matter

Structure 1.5.4—The relationship between the pressure, volume, temperature and amount of an ideal gas is shown in the ideal gas equation PV = nRT and the combined gas law P1V1T1= P2V2T2. Solve problems relating to the ideal gas equation.
